control_rationale: Handles malformed and transient/permanent send failures plus ack failure retries, but lacks a durable truth source for processed messages and therefore only weakly guarantees exactly-once-like behavior. No explicit operational verification coverage (metrics/tests), and retry/ack handling is less defensible under real queue edge cases; `Thread.sleep` retrying in handler is also operationally risky.
treatment_rationale: Strongest answer: introduces a durable ledger as source-of-truth, classifies malformed and permanent failures to DLQ, enforces bounded retries, and addresses ack-failure semantics with duplicate suppression via idempotent processing state. It also includes concrete observability and fault-injection style verification guidance.
judge_rationale: Response A is a stronger and more complete fix across fault handling and correctness boundaries. It is superior on state-of-truth design, idempotency guarantees, and verifiability, so it is the better answer.
